All right, so real quick, I wanted to get into the subject of voicemails very quickly here because Listen, this is a topic that I've been asked many times and I'll be frank with you I have never seen The leaving of voicemails Result in enough of a bump in this company sales that really make it worthy of turning it into a program I promise you if it was then I would do it. Yeah, maybe in 1958 or 1960 when answering machines probably first came out Maybe and even back at stratton days and it was a long time ago, right when people maybe would answer their voicemails Even then it was only marginally successful. Now I'll tell you flat out nowadays virtually no one Answers their looks at their voicemail on their phone like they don't they don't they look at the transcript for a second When no one picks up and listens to the whole thing I never even look at a voicemail like I listen if I see that I'm almost pissed off when someone leaves a voicemail And if you're under 30, I bet you're feeling like oh, why doesn't the person just text me if it mattered? So it almost creates animosity when someone leaves a voicemail So the fact that I see some things on the internet similarly if you don't leave a voicemail You've knocked yourself out of the box. You've pre-screened yourself out. That's just utter nonsense It's just not true. So please don't take that as being the parameter of what's like the proper procedure Always leave a voicemail or else not true. Now that being said If you want to leave a voicemail an experiment. Yeah, nothing wrong with that. You want to test it So I always have companies test it because remember it is an industry and company specific outcome If you're in the sort of field where people really want what you have and it's in short supply Yeah, voicemails can be effective by and large they're not but they can be effective So let me give you the rules for leaving a voicemail if you want to give it a shot And just so you know, they're far more effective when you do it in conjunction with also following up by email Fowling up with another phone call in other words. It's part of an overall strategy Not just let me leave a voicemail because I hope they'll call me back It's almost like it's part of the the marketing phenomena of let him see me on tv Hear my message on radio. Look at it in the newspaper See it on you get it and all of a sudden they hear it and see it four five six different places And they decide wow, maybe there's something going on here. So there's that aspect of it But just by and large just please just let me be honest There's nothing I can teach you that will Magically have everybody call you back because you leave voicemails In fact, I trained a very large logistics companies last year. We actually tested the leaving voicemails Versus not leaving voicemails and what we found the results of not leaving voicemails Were much better because it slowed the sales people down Invariably they made about 20% less calls when they left the voicemail every single time Okay, what would you say? Well for starters, if you are all going you got to keep it very short Quick boom boom 20 seconds tops. All right now you want to follow the same rules with tonality You never said hey, it's jordan belford course. Hey jordan belford here You want to use your phrasing of the clarifiers a question? You want to if you can include some type of hey have the answer to that um to that question you had or the solution you're looking for What you inquired about again doesn't fit every industry Keep it short sweet get right to the point leave your phone over at the end not in the beginning That's about the size of it now one more point here Before I give it to you. I want to get the ethics Commitment from here because this is one strategy that does work pretty well But I don't want you to be unethical about it. All right, that is if you're in certain businesses or industries where you're calling on service providers and if you leave the voicemail in a way in a tonality And the words are sort of vague enough that they'll think you're a customer Leaving a message You'll get a callback almost every single time you might have some angry people on your hands But you will get callback. So if you're calling up service providers like club plumbers electricians Even a haircut whatever it might be. Okay, and you're like, yeah, I'm calling about a plumbing job Do not say anything that's not correct I'm not telling you to do that what I'm saying is if you can find that little sweet spot like for instance with the Logistics company we tested this and it worked very well, but again It's still almost in that way Just straight on cold calling because you eventually you get through to people like by number, you know Calling five or six times at different times of the day So the other strategy I have for following up and getting through screens. You're more effective than this You get it sort of low on the totem pole, right? But you leave a voicemail, right? And what you would say in a logistic situation is hey, yeah, I'm calling about shipping a package I Mean I want to be the shipper But you said that to logistics manually. What was it a lost package? They'll call you up because I think it's a you know customer that maybe has an issue That's one way to get a call back. Okay, but again, please don't do anything unethical If it's in one of those situation where it fits you can do it But don't make it a habit. Okay, or so you don't say anything that is, you know over the line All right, so once again, you keep these messages very short You want to say your name but always phrase it in the form of a question Don't you say hi? I'm Jordan. Hey Jordan. But what so sort of the same rules of getting through the gatekeeper quick short to this point Phrasing the clarifier as a question. I have the answer. I have the solution you're looking for You're calling back this solution definitely works for you if it does so if that fits or have what you were looking for I'm calling about the thing you were you were looking for I just don't know what industry you're in, right? So Fashion it like that but very short Anything longer than 30 seconds. You're dead. You're gonna get pissed at you and again remember this I don't want to date the day of this chamber. We're like, you know 2019, right? So if you watch this in five years from now, you're probably fun that no one is answering voicemails at all All right, and certainly not in b to see if you're calling on individuals b to b in business You have a little bit more of a shot. Okay, so you could try those strategies. Remember the key here short concise Messages and test you want to test out leaving these messages versus leaving none at all and also Leaving messages but following up with also an email another call. Hey, I left your voicemail last week Yeah, I was in a voicemail about that thing So you can start combining these things together as different marketing and different modalities for getting through the gatekeeper There you go.
